headline: Pediatric Eye Doctor in Chennai
subheadline: Gentle, thorough eye care for children -- from routine vision screening to managing conditions present from birth -- with Dr. Rajeswari in KK Nagar.
metaDescription: Pediatric eye doctor in Chennai. Vision screening, squint evaluation, and child-friendly eye exams with Dr. Rajeswari in KK Nagar. Book an appointment.

whyPatientsChoose.title: Child-Friendly Approach
whyPatientsChoose.description: Exams paced for children, with clear explanations for parents at every step.

symptoms: Squinting, tilting the head, or covering one eye to see
Sitting very close to the TV or holding books close to the face
Eyes that don't appear to align (one drifts in or out)
Complaints of headaches or eye strain during reading or schoolwork

processSteps.title: Comfortable Evaluation
processSteps.description: Age-appropriate vision testing and eye examination, paced to keep your child comfortable.

processSteps.title: Clear Explanation for Parents
processSteps.description: We explain what we found in plain terms -- whether it's glasses, monitoring, or further treatment.

processSteps.title: Ongoing Care
processSteps.description: Children's eyes change as they grow -- we recommend a follow-up schedule appropriate to your child's needs.

landingFaqs.question: At what age should my child have their first eye checkup?
landingFaqs.answer: Even children with no apparent problems benefit from an early baseline checkup, and any visible signs -- squinting, head tilting, sitting close to screens -- warrant an evaluation regardless of age.

landingFaqs.question: Will the exam be stressful for my child?
landingFaqs.answer: Pediatric exams are paced specifically for children -- we take the time needed to keep your child comfortable rather than rushing through a standard adult protocol.

landingFaqs.question: My child's school did a vision screening already -- is a full eye exam still necessary?
landingFaqs.answer: School screenings are a useful first flag but aren't a substitute for a comprehensive exam, which checks eye alignment, eye health, and focusing ability in addition to basic vision -- things a screening often misses.

causeExploration.title: What Could Be Causing Your Child's Eye Symptoms?
causeExploration.causesIntro: Several common conditions can affect children's vision and eye comfort, many of which are very manageable when identified early:
commonCauses: Refractive errors (needing glasses) -- the most common cause of squinting or sitting close to screens
Strabismus (squint) -- eyes that don't align properly
Amblyopia ('lazy eye') -- reduced vision in one eye that develops in childhood
Eye strain from extended near-work or screen time
causeExploration.causesOutro: Most pediatric vision issues are highly treatable, especially when caught while a child's visual system is still developing.
causeExploration.urgentTitle: When to Seek a Prompt Evaluation
causeExploration.urgentIntro: Most concerns can wait for a scheduled appointment, but some signs warrant a sooner visit:
urgentScenarios: A noticeable misalignment of the eyes that has recently appeared
Sudden vision changes reported by your child
Eye injury or persistent redness with pain
A white reflection visible in your child's pupil in photographs

doctorsPerspectiveStructured.patientConcern: Parents often worry that a young child won't be able to cooperate with an eye exam, or that bringing them in over a minor concern is unnecessary.
doctorsPerspectiveStructured.response: Children's exams are built around them -- we adapt the approach to their age and comfort level. And it's always reasonable to bring in a concern that seems minor; many treatable conditions, like a lazy eye, have the best outcomes specifically when caught early in childhood.

learnMoreLink.text: Read our full guide to pediatric eye care
